zh.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn是Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster的女兒，於1556年7月26日在英國薩默塞特布盧頓受洗。她和Hugh Gaylord至少有8個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1629年2月17日之後在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特，埋葬在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Alwyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Alwin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Alwin or Elwin, a falling together of various Old English personal names: Ælfwine ‘elf friend’, Æthelwine ‘noble friend’, Ealdwine ‘old friend’, and others.
German: from a younger form of the personal name Adalwin (‘noble friend’), see above.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn was christened on 26 July 1556, in Bruton, Somerset, England as the daughter of Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster. She had at least 8 sons and 4 daughters with Hugh Gaylord. She died after 17 February 1629, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, and was buried in Pitminster, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn a été baptisée le 26 juillet 1556, à Bruton, Somerset, Angleterre, fille de Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster. Elle a eu au moins 8 fils et 4 filles avec Hugh Gaylord. Elle est décédée après 17 février 1629, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn wurde am 26. Juli 1556 in Bruton, Somerset, England, als Tochter von Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster getauft. Sie hatte mit Hugh Gaylord mindestens 8 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb nach 17. Februar 1629 in Pitminster, Somerset, England und wurde in Pitminster, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn è stato battezzato il 26 luglio 1556 nel Bruton, Somerset, England come figlia di Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster. Lei avuto almeno 8 figli e 4 figlie con Hugh Gaylord. Morì dopo il 17 febbraio 1629, nel Pitminster, Somerset, England, e fu sepolta a Pitminster, Somerset, England.

ja.Bruton, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトブルトン
ja.Pitminster, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトピトミンスター

ja.Pitminster, Somersetshire, England: イギリスイギリスソメルセトピトミンスター
ja.lifeSummary: Joan Alwynは1556年7月26日にイギリスソメルセトブルトンでGyles Alwyn of Pitminsterの娘として洗礼を受けた。彼女はHugh Gaylordとの間に少なくとも8人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1629年2月17日以後にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターで死去し，イギリスソメルセトピトミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn(은)는 1556년 7월 26일 영국 서머세트 브루턴에서 Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Hugh Gaylord(와)과 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1629년 2월 17일 이후 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.26 July 1556–after 17 February 1629: 26 de julho de 1556–após 17 de fevereiro de 1629
pt.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn foi batizada em 26 de julho de 1556, em Bruton, Somerset, Inglaterra filha de Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster. Ela teve pelo menos 8 filhos e 4 filhas com Hugh Gaylord. Ela faleceu após 17 de fevereiro de 1629, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn была крещена 26 июля 1556 г. в местности брутон, сомирсет, Англия. Её отца звали Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster. У неё было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 4 дочери от Hugh Gaylord. Она умерла после 17 февраль 1629 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия и была похоронена в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия.

es.26 July 1556–after 17 February 1629: 26 de julio de 1556–después de 17 de febrero de 1629
es.lifeSummary: Joan Alwyn se bautizó el 26 de julio de 1556, en Bruton, Somerset, Inglaterra como hija de Gyles Alwyn of Pitminster. Tuvo por lo menos 8 hijos y 4 hijas con Hugh Gaylord. Murió después de 17 de febrero de 1629, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra.
